rationale.root_cause: At the bad FLAW site (~line 33) data is set to the 50-byte dataBadBuffer, then the copy loop uses destLen = strlen(dest) (99) as the bound, causing reads past data[49]. The over-read occurs because the loop indexes data[i] up to 98 while the source buffer only has 50 bytes.
rationale.local_fix: Bound the loop by the source length (strlen(data)) capped at the destination capacity and then null-terminate dest. This prevents reading past the end of the source buffer.
rationale.global_fix: Redesign the API to pass explicit lengths/capacities with buffers (e.g., src length and dest capacity or a struct with ptr/len/cap). This avoids relying on sentinel-terminated raw buffers and prevents this class of over-read.
